Title: miR444b.2–HsfA1–AOC1 module mediates heat priming–enhanced blast resistance in rice
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Suppressed by 40°C pretreatment and by Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower under 40°C pretreatment than 22°C; inversely correlated with HsfA1 transcript abundance during blast infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates JA-associated heat-priming-enhanced blast resistance by repressing HsfA1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout; overexpression; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay; immunoblotting; site-directed mutagenesis of target site; RNA pull-down; RACE; polysome profiling; disease assay; fungal biomass quantification
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 40°C heat pretreatment for 24 h; Magnaporthe oryzae inoculation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. miR444b.2 is heat- and pathogen-responsive and is repressed by 40°C pretreatment. 2. miR444b.2 directly binds the second exon of HsfA1 mRNA, promoting cleavage and reducing translation. 3. Loss of miR444b.2 enhances blast resistance, whereas overexpression increases susceptibility, consistent with reduced JA pathway activation via HsfA1 repression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suppression or editing of miR444b.2/HsfA1 target pairing may improve heat-associated blast resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although a regulatory RNA, under the provided schema it is placed as a common gene because Regulation_Genes is intended for TF/signaling proteins; target-site-edited HsfA1 lines (mHsfA1) phenocopy reduced miR444b.2 repression.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms pathway intermediate required for JA production
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key JA biosynthesis gene in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Activated by HsfA1; reduced in hsfa1 mutant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Lower in hsfa1 mutants; promoter directly bound and activated by HsfA1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for heat-priming-enhanced JA accumulation and blast resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: knockout; overexpression in hsfa1 background; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; luciferase transactivation assay; disease assay; fungal biomass; JA quantification; promoter motif editing
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 40°C heat pretreatment for 24 h; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; exogenous MeJA rescue context
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AOC1 is a direct transcriptional target of HsfA1. 2. aoc1 mutants lose heat-priming-enhanced blast resistance. 3. AOC1 overexpression partially restores JA level and resistance in hsfa1 mutants, and editing the HsfA1-binding motif in the AOC1 promoter increases susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering heat-associated disease resistance; can be combined with HsfA1 activation or promoter optimization
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HI-LOX was also tested as a candidate JA gene but did not show the same functional requirement for heat-priming-enhanced blast resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1234290; RNA-seq hsfa1 vs ZH11: PRJNA1234552; CUT&Tag: PRJNA1234705
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native AOC1 promoter; edited promoter HsfA1-binding motif in mpAOC1 lines

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Heat shock transcription factor, HSF family
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Rapidly induced by 40°C treatment and induced by Magnaporthe oryzae infection
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher after 20 min heat treatment and at 72 hpi after infection; elevated in mir444b.2 knockout and reduced in miR444b.2 overexpression lines
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: 40°C heat priming; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; repression by miR444b.2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly activates AOC1 transcription; binds HI-LOX promoter in vitro, but AOC1 is the functionally decisive downstream target for resistance in this study
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense hormone mediating resistance to rice blast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates heat-priming-enhanced JA accumulation and blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive regulator linking heat sensing to JA-mediated blast resistance; loss abolishes heat-priming-enhanced JA accumulation and resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant phenotyping; overexpression; RNA-seq; CUT&Tag; RT-qPCR; yeast one-hybrid; EMSA; ChIP-qPCR; luciferase transactivation assay; disease assay; fungal biomass quantification; JA measurement; AOC1 overexpression rescue; MeJA rescue; uORF editing; natural haplotype analysis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 40°C pretreatment for 24 h; 22°C control; Magnaporthe oryzae infection; exogenous MeJA treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HsfA1 is the thermosensory-pathway factor specifically required for heat-priming-enhanced blast resistance. 2. HsfA1 directly activates AOC1 to promote JA biosynthesis, and hsfa1 mutants show reduced JA and increased susceptibility. 3. Natural favorable HsfA1 haplotypes and CRISPR uORF-edited lines elevate HsfA1 expression and improve blast resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for breeding and metabolic engineering through favorable allele selection, uORF editing, or release from miR444b.2 repression to enhance climate-resilient blast resistance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No significant effect on major agronomic traits was reported for knockout or overexpression lines, but hsfa1 mutants showed increased heat sensitivity; favorable lines aim to improve both heat tolerance and disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HsfA1 overexpression partially enhances resistance even under 22°C; promoter occupancy at AOC1 was not obviously altered by heat pretreatment or infection, indicating expression abundance rather than DNA-binding redistribution may be critical
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: PRJNA1234290; RNA-seq hsfa1 vs ZH11: PRJNA1234552; CUT&Tag: PRJNA1234705; miRNA-seq: PRJNA1234785
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; uORF-edited native 5' leader/promoter region in mu-HsfA1 lines
